hypothesis: Enhancing aromatic complementarity at position 5 and introducing hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 will improve SSTR2 selectivity by strengthening specific contacts with SSTR2-unique ECL2/ECL3 regions while avoiding off-target interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is effective for generating high-affinity binders but insufficient for achieving SSTR2 selectivity. Targeted mutations at non-pharmacophore positions are necessary to disrupt off-target interactions and improve Δmargin.

proposed_changes.rationale: To disrupt conserved-pocket contacts in SSTR3/SSTR5 while preserving pharmacophore positions (7-10) and Cys3/Cys14.
Introduce mutations at key non-pharmacophore positions to reduce off-target binding while maintaining native-like interactions at SSTR2.
